releaseNotes: Replaces opaque founder blockers with the Founder Question Contract: every gate names the current phase and outcome, defines unfamiliar terms, uses AskUserQuestion when available (the same two or three choices in plain text otherwise), puts one recommendation first, and states each route's consequence, next agent action, evidence effect, safe skip/fallback/defer process, and revisit trigger. Protected access/spend/legal/pricing/public/release/destructive decisions can defer but never bypass approval; silence remains pending; direct founder instructions supersede stale agent-created gates. The shipped research example specifically prevents a simple friend interview from becoming an unexplained compensation/recruitment/moderator/reviewer questionnaire.

Upgrades the business-access ledger/schema to 2.0.0 with current phase, nullable active gate, gate class/origin, definitions, choice options, bypass policy, safe work while waiting, lifecycle, and gate history; mirrors the contract into PROJECT_STATE, BUSINESS_ACCESS, the rendered launch cockpit, repo agent entrypoints, paid-tool routing, and artifact guidance. Adds migrate:founder-gates, which preserves legacy decision context, archives the old gate as stale, and re-presents it without carrying approval forward. check:founder-operator grows from 33 to 54 focused fixtures, including no-gate continuation, stale-choice removal, legacy readability/context, malformed cockpit input, option order/consequence floors, full-surface jargon detection, protected defer, bypass-route semantics, migration, and production cockpit reconciliation; founder-gate-jargon-without-choice becomes a required behavioral LaunchBench scenario.

Fixes source-worktree command resolution in the validator harness, LaunchBench, and Design Room renderer: root node_modules is two levels above skill/b2c-mobile-business-launch, not three. Fresh CE worktrees now execute their local tsx/vite binaries instead of incorrectly climbing into .worktrees/<branch>/node_modules and failing with ENOENT.

Makes founder-zero business operation the default: the agent assumes no business, provider, social, store, security, or launch expertise; explains what and why in plain language; presents exactly one founder action at a time; carries the full operating sequence; and continues into research, profile completion, content, support, analytics, store, and launch work after access clears. Ships references/founder-zero-operator.md, BUSINESS_ACCESS.md, a JSON Schema-backed operations/business-access.json ledger, top-level PROJECT_STATE business_operator state, continuity/orchestration/agent-entrypoint integration, a Business Operator Bootstrap cockpit and Control Plane panel, and explicit founder ownership of recovery, 2FA, money, legal identity, and final public/release decisions.

Adds check:founder-operator with thirty-three deterministic fixtures plus four LaunchBench scenarios and a live agent-behavior eval. The gate enforces a typed, single-target, five-part active founder gate; one-action sequencing; a non-terminal next business operation; canonical account kinds; founder-owned exact business assets; named operator identity; delegated-by and revocation paths; bidirectionally reconciled capability scopes; founder-controlled recovery and MFA; durable Doppler operator authentication; delegated social access before shared credentials; structured token exceptions; meaningful not-needed reasons; non-empty regular text proof; Agent Operations routing for binary evidence; expanded credential leakage detection including short MFA values; true-ready ownership/account resolution; and cross-artifact state reconciliation. Doppler is scoped to automation secrets such as API keys, OAuth/refresh tokens, service tokens, webhooks, store credentials, and CI integrations; browser passwords, passkeys, MFA codes, recovery codes, cookies, and sessions remain out of chat, repos, and Doppler. Current official guidance was refreshed for Doppler CLI/service tokens and delegated access on X, Meta, TikTok, and YouTube; local Doppler CLI behavior was verified at v3.76.0.

Adds Frontier Agent Operations as a cross-cutting execution contract for where frontier AI is now: current connector/API/CLI/browser/native capability discovery, exact provider/account/team/project/environment targeting, access-vs-authorization separation, expiring approval envelopes, observe/draft/mutate/publish/spend/release/destructive action classes, prompt-injection quarantine for pages/comments/videos/downloads, secret-safe signed-in-session use, sanitized before/after read-back proof, rollback/recovery, and canonical state reconciliation. Ships references/frontier-agent-operations.md, AGENT_OPERATIONS.md, a JSON Schema-backed operations/agent-operations.json ledger, top-level PROJECT_STATE agent_operations state, continuity/orchestration integration, and a planned agent-ops Control Plane panel.

Adds check:agent-operations with schema 1.1 and semantic enforcement: required connector/API-or-CLI/browser/native capability inventory; strict timestamps; exact capability/route/provider/account/team/project/environment/mode binding; operation/resource/payload/content scope; one-shot consumption; exclusions; spend ceilings; voice policy; authorization and evidence for failed risky attempts; secret scanning across ledger, human log, and text proof; grounded repo-relative artifacts; and cross-checks across PROJECT_STATE, AGENT_OPERATIONS, provider/canonical docs, and launch cockpit. Seventeen deterministic Agent Operations fixtures cover baseline and adversarial scope, time, capability, reconciliation, research-vs-operational browser reads, one-shot reuse, failed attempts, and secret leakage; five LaunchBench scenarios plus a live agent-behavior eval cover manual-handoff underreach, unapproved browser mutation, untraceable research, prompt injection, and stale state after action.

Hardens Apple/mobile truth from live local surfaces: fixes stale asc 0.38.1 examples (apps get, account/status, review submissions-list, diff localizations, and both validate version forms), adds check:asc-command-contract with optional installed-help verification and regression fixtures, updates MobAI guidance to current 1.9.3 device discovery/selectors/screenshot/wait/web/metrics/transition-recording routes, and deepens native iOS proof with a launch-critical .xctestplan matrix across unit/integration/UI/performance, device/OS/locale/appearance, accessibility, permissions, resilience, StoreKit/RevenueCat lifecycle, performance, physical-device Release coverage, and existing on-disk evidence for done engineering.

Upgrades RESEARCH.md and check:research for multimodal frontier research: canonical URL/source ID, observed timestamp, tool/backend/query, transcript and visual provenance, sampling limits, observation-vs-inference, confidence, artifact/trace impact, agent-reach read-only routing, and untrusted-content quarantine. Registers the new official Apple testing, test-plan, accessibility-audit, localization-test, and performance-test sources for weekly freshness. Refreshes Vite 6.4.2 to patched 6.4.3 and the locked esbuild transitive to 0.27.7; npm audit is clean in both source and runtime packages. Excludes machine-local .claude worktrees from npm packages so maintainer agent state cannot leak into the published tarball.

Ships the Landing Motion Craft lane — the abstraction PR #23's brainstorm scoped and deferred to a founder decision (docs/brainstorms/landing-motion-craft.md; founder greenlit the full change-set). New references/landing-motion-craft.md carries the two-lane content model (Remotion bakes hero loops/section media; motion/react owns live scroll/hover/cursor choreography — both reading one promoted token scale), the section catalog, the enforceable progressive-enhancement contract (real text always, reduced-motion collapse, no LCP gating, tokens not magic numbers), and the production floor (self-hosted webfonts, opt-in license-gated baked hero video, optional Lenis with registry registration). Routed from geo-seo.md 3a and design-visual-system.md; motionsites.ai registered in source-registry.yaml as an inspiration benchmark (never a command-syntax source).

Pre-merge adversarial verification (three review lenses, 13 agents, 10 confirmed findings, 0 rejected) hardened the lane before release: Bento/Testimonials/Pricing no longer hide real copy via motion's SSR-baked initial prop (they now use the js-gated .lm-reveal CSS mechanism or gate hiding initials on the new useHydratedMotionGate() hook, which also removes the useReducedMotion()-null SSR/hydration mismatch for reduced-motion users); readMotionTokens() is memoized via useMotionTokens(); Cta's morph class relies on the CSS reduced-motion collapse instead of a mismatching JS branch. check-landing-funnel's motion detection no longer fires on prose ('transition:' in .mdx counts only with real CSS/JS animation markers; 'transition: none' is disabling, not animating), the reduced-motion presence scan is repo-wide (styles/globals.css satisfies it), JS-only animation (camelCase transitionDuration/animationDuration, gsap, .animate()) is detected, and landing-site scope also recognizes landing/package.json|app|pages. The template-safety landing exception is anchored to the top-level landing/ segment. references/landing-motion-craft.md now states honestly that rules 1/2/4 are mechanically enforced while rule 3 (LCP) is architectural guidance; the brainstorm doc's status line records the lane as implemented. Six regression fixtures pin the fixes; test:validators 225 -> 231.

templates/landing/ is the runnable section library: eight typed motion/react sections (Hero with mesh/baked-video slot + scroll parallax + hydration-gated word stagger, Marquee, Bento, sticky Scrollytelling, count-up Stats, spotlight Testimonials, glass Pricing with live billing toggle, gradient-morph CTA), lib/motion-tokens.ts (SSR-safe --motion-* reader + the html.js gate), and motion.css (js-gated reveal utilities that hide nothing without JavaScript, marquee keyframes, and the prefers-reduced-motion collapse). Aesthetic-neutral by design; hosts are the Next.js archetype starters or any React SSR site. check-template-safety gains a deliberate, documented exception: motion/react is mandated inside landing/ (web-only surface) and still banned everywhere else in templates — both sides fixture-pinned.

Motion-token promotion: state/theme.tokens.json (and the templates copy) gain the landing scale — durationReveal 600ms, durationCinematic 1200ms, easingEmphasis (expo-out), easingSpring, stagger 60ms — promoted by promote-design-tokens.ts into tokens.css/tokens.json/DesignTokens.swift on both roots (new token hash; design rooms re-rendered) and enforced by check-token-promotion's motion-var list, so Remotion compositions and live landing motion share one brand timing system.

check-landing-funnel grows a Landing Motion Craft gate set, active when landing sources animate: landing_funnel.motion.reduced_motion_missing (error — no prefers-reduced-motion/useReducedMotion handling anywhere), landing_funnel.motion.hero_text_not_static (error — animated landing HTML whose h1 carries no static text), and landing_funnel.motion.untokenized_duration (warning, error at done — raw duration literals without var(--motion-*)). Scope detection is simultaneously tightened to site-shaped signals (landing/index.html, public/, wrangler.toml, or an explicit lane status): a copied-in section library alone no longer triggers the deploy gates, and the audit-plan exclusion reason is updated to match. Six new fixtures (compliant animated site passes; each motion gate fails; pack-only root stays out of scope; landing template-safety exception) and the landing-motion-progressive-enhancement-missing LaunchBench scenario lock the contract. test:validators grows 219 -> 225.

Verification-pass fixes: an adversarial review workflow over the six deep-audit PRs (v0.12.2..v0.14.0) confirmed five defects, all now fixed with regression fixtures. (1) check-live-provider-proof's requiresProof free-text trigger had no negation awareness, so the shipped PRODUCTION_READINESS.md template's own cautionary boilerplate ('Do not mark this app launch-ready until ...') hard-failed early repos where no lane was done and PROVIDER_PROOF.md had not been seeded yet (reproduced); readiness prose alone now yields a warning, and the hard error stays scoped to done proof-required lanes. (2) The Proof Ledger parser split rows on raw '|' and read the evidence path at a fixed column, so a literal pipe in the proof-command cell (curl ... | jq ...) shifted every later column and produced false evidence_path errors; the whole row is now scanned for path tokens. (3) Evidence paths containing spaces were silently truncated; backtick-quoted paths are now parsed as single tokens and provider-proof.md documents the convention.

(4) check-backend-data-contract's Authorization Model extraction required the heading to start exactly with the section name, so numbered headings ('### 3. Authorization Model') silently widened the scan to the whole spec and let an unrelated 'tested' elsewhere satisfy the authz gate; the matcher now tolerates numbering/decoration and #### depth. (5) audit-skill-links orphan reachability used raw substring containment, so a basename that is a substring of another mentioned file's basename ('lane.md' inside 'sub-lane.md') was falsely reachable; matching is now boundary-aware (a preceding path separator still counts, since a longer path ending in the needle references the same file). Also hardens check-revenue's extractRevenueCatEvidencePath (same pre-existing naive-split pattern, confirmed reproducible): the positional evidence cell is only accepted when it actually looks like a file path, otherwise the row-scan fallback runs. Four regression fixtures pin the reproductions; test:validators grows 215 -> 219.

Closes the four-lane validator gap found by the deep audit: of the 22 required lanes, product, privacy_legal, traceability, and research had no dedicated content validator — only the generic lane-coverage status floor saw them, so SPEC.md/PRIVACY.md/TERMS.md/LAUNCH_TRACE.md/RESEARCH.md could be one-line stubs behind a done lane. Four new validators (check:research / check-research-evidence.ts, check:product-spec, check:launch-trace, check:privacy-terms) enforce each artifact's structural floor from references/artifact-contracts.md: file required unless the lane is not_needed/deferred, required sections as warnings that harden to errors at done, template-placeholder ('replace with', YYYY-MM-DD, TODO/TBD) done-guards, dated-evidence requirement for done research, and TRACE-<n> ID + RESEARCH/SPEC linkage for the trace. Wired into both package script sets, lib/audit-plan.ts (audit grows 56 -> 60 steps), and run-launchbench knownValidators; the artifact contracts now name their validators inline.

Enriches the five thin starter templates into contract-complete skeletons so the shipped baseline passes the new floors with zero warnings: RESEARCH.md gains Source Ledger columns (source type, tool/query, confidence, artifact), a Decision Log with trace IDs, and Rejected Claims; SPEC.md gains 11-Star Experience, Category And Competitors, Core Product Loop, Monetization Posture, Metrics, and Risks And Open Questions sections (and now references 11_STAR_EXPERIENCE.md and RESEARCH.md, strengthening the eleven-star done-linkage check); LAUNCH_TRACE.md gains Decision Trace, Rejected Decisions, Founder-Only Decisions, and Blockers sections; PRIVACY.md gains effective/updated dates, entity and privacy contact, Retention And Deletion, and App Store Disclosure Mapping; TERMS.md gains effective date, Subscriptions And Refunds, Disclaimers And Liability, and Platform Terms. Thirteen new fixtures pin baseline-pass, done-with-placeholders, missing-artifact, and a fully-populated done-research pass; test:validators grows 202 -> 215.

Wires check-native-ios-proof into the audit pipeline as check:native-ios (both package script sets plus a lib/audit-plan.ts step with the standard template-state args) — docs/skill-workflow-loops.md claimed it ran 'via npm run audit' and 'passes under audit:ci', but the fully-implemented, fixture-covered 216-line validator had no npm script binding and was absent from the audit plan, so the native iOS proof gate never actually executed. The doc claim is now true instead of corrected away. Also registers render-business-control-plane-workspace in run-launchbench knownValidators (a LaunchBench scenario could not even cite the 488-line workspace gate before) and adds pass/drift fixtures for its --check mode.

Adds the SKILL.md entrypoint context budget to check-reference-size: SKILL.md loads on every trigger yet was the only shipped context file with no size gate (references get 64KiB each). The new ENTRYPOINT_BUDGET_BYTES ceiling (68KiB) sits just above the current size, so any addition must be paid for by subtraction (freeze-and-subtract); ratchet it down as SKILL.md shrinks. Two confirmed near-verbatim duplications inside SKILL.md were subtracted (the Runtime Freshness Gate paragraph now defers to skill-versioning.md's Runtime Freshness Loop instead of restating it; the Operating Posture PROJECT_STATE bullet defers to the Deliverable Standard's field enumeration instead of repeating it), taking SKILL.md 69,670 -> 69,472 bytes. An oversized-SKILL.md fixture pins the gate; test:validators grows 199 -> 202.

Link-graph enforcement: audit-skill-links.ts grows from a markdown-link checker into a three-gate integrity check. Gate 2 (skill_links.orphan_file) errors on any file under references/ or templates/ that no other shipped file mentions by relative path, basename, or ancestor directory — dead weight no agent can route to; basename and ancestor-dir matching keep validator-constructed paths (path.join(root, 'tokens.json')) and copy-the-directory scaffolds (archetype starter/) from false-failing, and a reasoned ORPHAN_EXCLUSIONS map exists for deliberate exceptions. Gate 3 (skill_links.duplicate_template) errors on byte-identical files under templates/ outside the archetype starters, where cross-pack duplication is the design. Four fixtures pin all three gates (the previously-unfixtured broken-link gate included).

Removes templates/orchestration/ — a byte-identical, never-referenced duplicate of templates/ORCHESTRATION.md and templates/orchestration.html that the new duplicate gate would now catch (validators keep accepting orchestration/ORCHESTRATION.md as an alternate app-repo location; the skill just no longer ships the same bytes twice). The deep-audit's other orphan candidates were verified live and kept: templates/design-room.html and templates/design-system/tokens.* are load-bearing for the audit baseline via constructed paths (check-design-room-contract render-hash check, check-token-promotion --root templates).

Closes the revenuecat example-copy evasion: check-revenue's byte-identity floor compared the proof only against the app repo's revenue/revenuecat-proof.example.json, so pasting the shipped example's content as proof evaded detection whenever the app repo never seeded the example. The floor now also compares against the example shipped inside the skill itself; a fixture pins the unseeded-repo case. references/app-store-listing-prep.md now routes templates/app-store-listing/SCREENSHOT_RUBRIC.md by path (previously only bare-filename mentions, so nothing told an agent where to seed the rubric from). test:validators grows 194 -> 199.

Words-not-work hardening, round one (from the adversarially verified deep-audit backlog): three done-gates that pure prose could satisfy now demand on-disk work. check-live-provider-proof grounds the PROVIDER_PROOF.md Proof Ledger once a mapped lane (PostHog->analytics_attribution, RevenueCat->revenue, Resend->email, App Store Connect->store_console/apple_signing, Sentry->security) is done: the provider row must exist, its current-status cell must read as captured evidence (needs/pending/planned wording fails), and at least one path named in the evidence-path cell must exist on disk — keyword presence alone no longer passes. MobAI/Doppler rows are deliberately unmapped (engineering/secrets proof have their own validators and legitimately route through non-MobAI tooling).

check-paid-user-acquisition adds a content floor to the done-state blended report: growth/paid-ua-report.csv (or .md) must carry a header naming spend plus a downstream economics column (CPA/LTV/revenue/ROAS/payback) and at least one data row — the previous check accepted any non-empty file, so a one-line stub could mark the lane done. check-backend-data-contract grounds the done-gate authorization claim: the Authorization Model must state the rules are tested and point at at least one artifact that exists on disk (RLS test file, migration, or proof doc); a bare 'RLS' anywhere in TECH_SPEC.md prose no longer passes.

references/provider-proof.md, backend-data-contract.md, and paid-user-acquisition.md document the new grounding contracts next to the ledger/report/authz sections they gate. Six new fixtures pin the behavior (grounded pass, missing evidence artifact, still-planned ledger status, stub report, untested prose-only RLS, tested claim without artifact); test:validators grows 188 -> 194.

Fixes a false-pass exit bug in three validators' scope-skip paths: check-google-play-readiness.ts, check-post-launch-ops.ts, and check-landing-funnel.ts called process.exit(0) immediately after reportAndExit(), which stomped the process.exitCode = 1 that reportAndExit sets — so on a missing or invalid PROJECT_STATE.yaml each printed '1 error(s) ... project_state.missing' and still exited 0 (reproduced before the fix). All seven scope-skip sites that used process.exit(0) (also check-analytics-catalog, check-aso-metadata, check-email, check-localization-research, where the false-pass was not reachable but the pattern was identical) now use argument-less process.exit(), matching the correct sites that already existed later in the same files; regression fixtures pin the three reproducible cases.

Deepens only-pass/skip-only fixture coverage found by the adversarial audit: check-landing-funnel gains in-scope pass and in-scope fail fixtures (its single prior fixture only exercised the not-in-scope early return, so the five deploy gates, GEO/SEO static-file checks, and copy guardrails had zero coverage); check-version-discipline gains manifest-missing and malformed-manifest (semver/updatedAt) fail fixtures; validate-state gains a missing-state-files fail fixture. test:validators grows 180 -> 188 cases.

Fixture coverage for the eight previously-unfixtured gates (8 -> 0 zero-fixture validators; test:validators grows from 172 to 180 cases). check-agent-entrypoints, check-autopilot-contract, check-workflow-adherence, check-package-parity, and check-template-safety get pass+fail cases in the new scripts/fixtures/repo-gates.fixtures.ts (the package-parity pair is synthesized from lib/audit-plan.ts itself so plan drift keeps the fixture honest); check-launch-narrative (missing narrative, hashtag in fenced launch copy), check-revenue (done lane without the live revenuecat-proof.json probe artifact), and check-lane-coverage (required lane deleted from PROJECT_STATE.yaml) get pass+fail cases in their domain fixture modules. Until now the audit pipeline only ever exercised these gates' PASS paths, so a regression that made any of them always-pass was invisible; every gate now has at least one failing input proving it can fail.

Carries the two v0.11.1 validator fixes forward onto v0.12.0 (the #22 PR branched from the pre-0.11.1 base and shipped without them); rebased onto v0.12.0 and re-cut as 0.12.1 with version parity across skill-version.json, both package.json files, and both lockfiles.

Fixes check-store-screenshots PNG IHDR parsing under the tsx ESM runtime: parsePngIhdr used require("node:fs") inside an ESM context, so every IHDR parse failed with "require is not defined" and emitted false png_ihdr_unreadable findings; node:fs functions are now imported at module top.

Corrects check-aso-metadata screenshot folder/dimension cross-flag against current ASC reality: APP_IPHONE_67 and APP_IPHONE_69 accept identical dimension lists (verified live 2026-06-10 via asc screenshots sizes — both wells take 1260x2736, 1290x2796, and 1320x2868), so a 6.9-inch asset in an iphone-67 well is legitimate. The error is now a warning (aso_metadata.screenshot_folder_dimension_mixed) and is skipped when the doc records the authoritative live read (asc screenshots list), which surfaced as 13 false errors against the live, Apple-approved Clueless listing.

Converts both shipped archetype packs (social-network, ai-chat-companion) from prompt packs into runnable starter scaffolds under templates/app-archetypes/<pack>/starter/: Next.js 16 App Router + Supabase pre-wired per current official docs (proxy.ts session refresh — middleware.ts was renamed in Next 16; @supabase/ssr browser/server clients), magic-link auth, schema migrations with enabled RLS and pgTAP tests exercising owner access plus cross-user/anonymous denial (tested RLS per backend-data-contract.md), Stripe checkout/webhook and RevenueCat webhook→entitlement stubs, PostHog wiring with a typed snake_case event catalog carrying the analytics lane's canonical events, names-only .env.example with every secret-shaped name pre-routed in templates/secrets/SECRETS.md, a CI workflow (typecheck + build on placeholder public env), pinned dependencies with committed lockfiles (both starters typecheck and `next build` green at authoring), and a README mapping every prompt to the scaffold area it customizes — prompts remain the customization layer. The ai-chat starter streams Claude server-side with the model id in ANTHROPIC_MODEL (resolved via the claude-api skill, never memory). Design decisions (versioning, sync, validation) recorded in docs/brainstorms/archetype-starter-scaffolds.md.

Adds the check-archetype-starter deterministic validator (structure completeness incl. lockfile and pinned core deps, names-only .env.example with required names, no real-looking secret patterns or committed .env files, RLS enabled + policies + test file present, snake_case event catalog with canonical + pack core-loop events, prompt→scaffold map covering every prompt, and backend adaptability stated per backend-data-contract.md), registered in scripts/lib/audit-plan.ts, both package.json script sets, and run-launchbench knownValidators; extends check-app-archetype so shipped packs must route to their starter. Covered by pass/fail fixtures (scripts/fixtures/archetype.fixtures.ts: missing lockfile, env value committed, missing RLS tests) and the archetype-starter-ignored LaunchBench scenario; SKILL.md step 1a, both lane references, and both pack READMEs route the starter as the build floor.

Adds the live behavioral eval harness — the execution layer run-launchbench deliberately lacks. scripts/run-behavioral-evals.ts runs the opt-in flagship subset (behavioral: true in evals/launchbench and evals/agent-behavior; currently 8 scenarios incl. stale-installed-skill-runtime, live-provider-proof-missing + provider-proof-before-ready, post-launch-ops-runbook-missing, launch-tier-overproduction, monetization-cozy-default-stack-unexamined, archetype-starter-ignored, ce-unavailable-standalone-loop-missing) against a live Claude agent primed with SKILL.md, grades must_catch/should_say/must_use/forbidden via a structured-output grader call, and writes a JSON results artifact; model ids default to claude-opus-4-8 per the claude-api skill (2026-06-10), overridable, never date-suffixed. Triggered by the new manually-dispatched behavioral-evals GitHub Actions workflow (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret, artifact upload) and `npm run evals:behavioral` in both packages — deliberately OUT of the PR-gating audit pipeline (cost/model flake); the honest split is documented in references/launchbench-evals.md and SKILL.md. The launchbench lint now validates the behavioral field and pins the flagship five; deterministic surfaces (--list, missing-credential gate) carry fixtures, and the behavioral-eval-split-misclaimed LaunchBench scenario locks the honesty contract. Adds @anthropic-ai/sdk as a devDependency.

Adds two new shipped archetype packs following the full pack pattern. habit-tracker (habit/streak/daily-routine/wellness utilities): references/habit-tracker-lane.md with the batched AskUserQuestion shape (habit model, surface — honest native-is-home note, optional systems, niche), dependency-ordered prompts 00-07 plus wellness-coach and simple-counter-utility (lite-tier) variants, timezone-correct streak engine guidance, and the Streak/Loss-Aversion HIGH-risk ethics contract threaded throughout (free recovery escape hatch, streak_recovered counter-metric, no guilt copy). photo-ai-media (AI avatars/headshots, photo enhance/restore, AI art studios): references/photo-ai-media-lane.md with the media-shape AskUserQuestion, prompts 00-08 (content safety & rights is a launch gate) plus avatar-headshot-studio and photo-restoration variants, the generation provider deliberately founder-gated via paid-tool-routing/TOOL_DECISIONS with a server-side MEDIA_GENERATION_API_KEY, metering-before-monetization, and the Variable Reward HIGH-risk contract on the reveal. Both packs thread the 11-star, emotional-design/ethics, revenue (SOSA 2026 defaults to test), analytics, and security lanes; both carry agent-behavior evals, SKILL.md step 1a + When-To-Load routing, and check-app-archetype coverage.

Both new packs ship runnable starter scaffolds reusing the v0.12.0 starter pattern (check:archetype-starter coverage, pinned deps + lockfiles, typecheck and next build green at authoring): habit-tracker starter carries profiles-with-timezone/habits/habit_checkins/streak_recoveries/reminders with tested owner-only RLS and the streak-recovery escape hatch in schema and event catalog; photo-ai-media starter carries media_assets/generations/usage_events plus an owner-scoped private storage bucket with policies, a provider-agnostic metered generation route with moderation hooks, and the media event catalog.

Context-budget pass. references/experience-cards.md (~200KB) is split into a ~9KB index (card shape, summary table, Ethics Ladder, and a new Card Routing table with one-line load-when triggers and risk tiers) plus twelve self-contained per-card files under references/experience-cards/ (~15-20KB each) so agents load only the cards in scope; emotional-design-system.md spec pointers, SKILL.md step 16a, and the When-To-Load rule now route through the index (check:emotional-design reads business artifacts, not the reference, so its phrase contracts are unaffected — verified against its source). SKILL.md's When To Load section is deduplicated against Start Here where lines repeated verbatim (secrets, security, emotional-design entries now carry triggers and point at the step that owns the full contract). Adds the check:reference-size validator: a generous 64KB per-file budget over references/**/*.{md,yaml} with an explicit reasoned exclusion list (tool-recipes.md, artifact-contracts.md, source-registry.yaml) and a stale-exclusion warning when an excluded file shrinks back under budget — registered in the audit plan, both package script sets, knownValidators, with pass/fail fixtures and the context-budget-exceeded LaunchBench scenario.

Thin-lane validator deepening. check:email now goes beyond template presence: EMAIL_OPS.md must reference the sender-domain DNS records (SPF and DKIM — the documented basis for proof/email-spf-dkim-pass.*), document unsubscribe handling, and record the DESIGN.md brand-token mapping per the templates/resend/email-templates.ts LaunchEmailDesignSystem contract (warning at partial, error at done, on top of the existing proof artifacts and Doppler-routed RESEND_API_KEY); templates/EMAIL_OPS.md gains the Brand Tokens mapping table. New check:analytics-catalog validator reconciles events across docs: every event named in ONBOARDING.md, EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md, or VIRAL_GROWTH.md (narrow extraction — backticked tokens in event/analytics table columns and bare event bullets, so property names never count) must exist in ANALYTICS.md's catalog, warning at partial and error when lanes.analytics_attribution is done; templates/ANALYTICS.md's Event Contract and Emotion Card Events tables gain the 18 events its sibling docs already named (onboarding steps, paywall/purchase/entitlement, the viral loop set, commitment_echoed, variable_reward_anticipation_started, peak_moment_reached) so the shipped templates reconcile. Both registered in the audit plan, both package script sets, and knownValidators; covered by paired pass/fail fixtures (email done with/without the three contracts; analytics done reconciled vs drifted), the new email-lane-deep-proof-missing LaunchBench scenario, and check-analytics-catalog wired into the existing onboarding-event-invented-without-catalog scenario.

Adds the Post-Launch Operations lane so the skill covers the live business, not just the path to approval. New references/post-launch-operations.md (weekly operating rhythm, crash triage with a named route and crash-free release gate, review responses with an SLA and review mining, release/hotfix cadence with staged rollout and rollback posture, retention review wired to the SOSA renewal/churn findings, support operations, and the launch retro loop), new templates/POST_LAUNCH_OPS.md and templates/LAUNCH_RETRO.md, a new required post_launch_ops lane in PROJECT_STATE.yaml/requiredLanes, a new phase_6b in launch-phases.md and the phase order, and the check-post-launch-ops validator (runbook + sections when the lane is claimed or the project is post-launch; done additionally requires a named crash route, a review-response SLA, a retention cohort source, and LAUNCH_RETRO.md). Covered by lifecycle fixtures, the post-launch-ops-runbook-missing LaunchBench scenario, and a post-launch-ops-live-app agent-behavior eval.

Adds Google Play parity as a first-class lane instead of a passing mention. New references/google-play-release.md (developer account and verification with the personal-account 12-tester/14-day closed-testing production gate planned from kickoff, app record and content declarations, Data Safety reconciled with the iOS privacy labels, IARC content rating, Play App Signing with the AAB requirement, the yearly-ratcheting target API level checked at release time, release tracks with staged rollout, pre-launch report triage, Play Billing/RevenueCat reconciliation, and policy/appeals), new templates/GOOGLE_PLAY_RELEASE.md, and the check-google-play-readiness validator gated on Android scope (platforms/bundle id) — packet required as the store lane hardens; done additionally requires the AAB route, the closed-testing gate addressed, and Data Safety reconciliation stated. Three official Play URLs registered in source-registry.yaml; covered by fixtures and the google-play-parity-skipped LaunchBench scenario.

Adds the backend data contract so builds stop coupling to the archetype's Supabase default by inertia. New references/backend-data-contract.md (Supabase/Firebase/custom backend selection recorded with a reason, portable data model with PII/retention/deletion paths, the same authorization matrix expressed as RLS vs Firestore security rules vs middleware authz with deny-by-default and tested-access proof, migrations and environments, API surface and client sync, and an archetype-prompt parity table for non-Supabase routes), a new Data Contract section in templates/TECH_SPEC.md, and the check-backend-data-contract validator (Data Contract section + sub-sections when TECH_SPEC.md exists; engineering done additionally requires a named backend route and a named authorization enforcement). Covered by fixtures and the backend-data-contract-missing LaunchBench scenario.

Makes CE-unavailable a real fallback path instead of a recorded excuse: engineering-orchestration.md gains the Standalone Engineering Loop (§1b — plan to the full §6 contract, bounded slices, adversarial review where the producer is never the only reviewer, test, proof — each stage mapped to its CE equivalent), compound-engineering-routing.md and templates/ENGINEERING_PLAN.md reference it, and check-compound-engineering-routing now errors when compound_engineering.availability is unavailable (or the route is ce_fallback) and ENGINEERING_PLAN.md does not record the loop. Covered by fixtures and the ce-unavailable-standalone-loop-missing LaunchBench scenario.

Adds launch tiers so the artifact burden scales with the product: project.launch_tier (full default / lite) is confirmed with the founder at orient, documented in launch-phases.md (Launch Tiers) and project-state.md, validated by validate-project-state (only full/lite accepted), and threaded through SKILL.md (Start Here step 2, Operating Posture, Default Phase Flow, Deliverable Standard). Lite defers the named breadth lanes through the normal deferral mechanics with dated reasons — the tier never silently disables a validator, revenue/privacy/security/signing/store lanes are never deferred by tier, and deferred lanes revisit at the day-30 retro. Covered by a fixture and the launch-tier-overproduction LaunchBench scenario.

Maintainer-audit hardening: replaces the two ~4,300-char duplicated audit/audit:ci shell chains with scripts/run-audit.ts, an orchestrator over a single declarative step list in scripts/lib/audit-plan.ts (typecheck barrier first, independent gates through a small concurrency pool with output flushed in plan order, launchbench serialized, --ci/--list/--only/--serial/--concurrency flags). check-package-parity now verifies plan coverage structurally: every check:*/validate:* script must be an audit-plan step or an explicitly excluded script with a recorded reason (check:landing-funnel, check:source-freshness alias, test:validators), every plan step must resolve to a real script in both packages, and both audit entrypoints must route through run-audit.ts — so a gate can no longer be silently dropped by editing one of several near-identical strings.

Shared CLI kernel in scripts/lib/launch-state.ts (expandHome, spec-driven parseFlags with path/string/number/boolean kinds and strict mode, flag accessors, normalizedIncludes, missingPhraseCode, normalizedStringArray); 13 private parseArgs implementations and duplicated helpers across the validators are migrated to it with byte-identical CLI semantics (env defaults, conditional --root fallback, strict-value errors, and mode derivation all preserved; check-parallel-orchestration keeps its space-only missingPhraseCode because the shared version would change emitted issue codes).

Splits the 2,083-line run-validator-fixtures.ts into a 45-line entrypoint plus scripts/fixtures/ harness, state helpers, builder modules, and seven domain fixture modules registered in original execution order (every fixture label byte-identical); the temp fixture root is now removed in a finally block (opt out with --keep-temp), fixing unbounded /tmp growth.

New fixture coverage for previously-untested surfaces: probe-posthog/probe-revenuecat no-credential founder paths, four grade-screenshots fixtures (task-template emit, missing grading input, builder==grader rejection, separate-pass ledger write), and six hook fixtures that execute the shipped settings.json PostToolUse commands with sample CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT payloads (blocking final-PNG gates with and without SKILL_ROOT, benign pass-through, loud jq/SKILL_ROOT warnings). Fixes a latent ESM bug in grade-screenshots.ts where require("node:fs") made the PNG IHDR dimension read unreachable under tsx.

Shipped business-repo hooks (templates/repo-agent-entrypoints/settings.json) no longer fail silently: a missing jq or unset SKILL_ROOT now prints a loud WARN to stderr and exits 0, while the blocking producer-vs-verifier screenshot gates keep firing without SKILL_ROOT; the setup comment documents the loud failure modes.

Honest eval naming: run-launchbench output and references/launchbench-evals.md now state that the gate is a scenario definition lint plus the deterministic validator-fixture suite — scenario prompts are not executed against a live agent. launchbench-evals.md gains a Validator Phrase Contracts section documenting the literal-vocabulary design (validator source is the contract), the gaming/false-negative edges, and the most-tripped literal tokens (review_prompt_eligible/review_prompt_requested, 2026 launch-copy guardrails, attribution event names, screenshots phrasing).

CI and supply-chain hygiene: the source-freshness workflow defaults to contents: read with write scopes re-granted only to the weekly refresh job, and all GitHub Actions (checkout, setup-node, upload-artifact, peter-evans/create-pull-request) are pinned to full commit SHAs with version comments. refresh-source-freshness.ts replaces the strictly-sequential fetch loop with a per-host-serialized concurrency pool (5 workers) so ~190 sources refresh in minutes while staying polite per docs site.

Formatting is now enforced: Prettier (printWidth 160, double quotes, trailing commas) with lint:format/format scripts in both packages, a one-time mechanical reformat of scripts/**/*.ts, and a lint:format step in the audit plan so CI fails on drift.

Docs truth pass: root AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md runtime-sync instructions are now explicitly maintainer-machine-conditional (clones/CI skip runtime sync; npm run audit:ci is the gate), README declares Node 22 (matching CI and CONTRIBUTING), the README layout includes evals/agent-behavior/ and evals/triggering/, and the Validate section documents the run-audit orchestrator and the plan-coverage guarantee.

Adds the second shipped archetype pack: the AI Chat / Companion build lane. New references/ai-chat-companion-lane.md detects AI-chat products (assistant, companion/character, or domain copilot), confirms shape via AskUserQuestion (chat type, surface, modality, memory depth, safety scope), and ships templates/app-archetypes/ai-chat-companion/ with copy-paste prompts (positioning, conversations+usage+memory schema, auth, streaming chat core loop, server-side Claude API model integration with context management, long-term memory, usage metering, Stripe monetization, and a required safety/moderation pass) plus companion-character and voice-first variants. The lane keeps the Claude API key server-side, sources current model IDs from the claude-api skill, treats safety/age-gating as a launch gate, and threads into the 11-star, emotional-design/ethics, revenue, security, and privacy lanes. Generalizes check-app-archetype to enforce both shipped packs' wiring, adds an ai-chat-companion agent-behavior eval, and wires SKILL.md step 1a + a When-To-Load rule.

Adds the check-app-archetype deterministic validator and an app-archetype-pack-incomplete LaunchBench scenario so the archetype layer is gated, not prose-only. The validator enforces that every pack under templates/app-archetypes/ is structurally complete (README + prompts/ with fenced copy-paste blocks), that the shipped social-network pack carries its required core prompts (schema, auth, feed, profiles), and that the lane is wired into references/social-network-lane.md, SKILL.md routing, and the agent-behavior eval. Wired into both package.json audit and audit:ci chains, registered in run-launchbench knownValidators, and covered by pass/fail fixtures in run-validator-fixtures.

Adds the app-archetype prompt-pack layer, seeded with the Social / Community Platform build lane. New references/social-network-lane.md detects when the founder is building a social product (X/Instagram/TikTok-style or a niche community app), confirms the shape via AskUserQuestion (content format text/image/video-first, primary surface web vs native, optional systems, niche), records it in PROJECT_STATE.yaml, and routes a reusable boilerplate prompt pack into the build instead of improvising schema and feed. It frames the five core systems (identity, content+storage, social graph, feed, engagement+notifications), a dependency-ordered build sequence, and threads each piece back into the existing research, 11-star, design, security (RLS), revenue, growth, and analytics lanes rather than bypassing them.

Ships templates/app-archetypes/social-network/ — a copy-paste-ready prompt pack (00 niche strategy on Claude.ai, 01 database schema + RLS + seed, 02 auth, 03 feed+posts core loop, 04 profiles+follow+notifications, 05 search/discovery, 06 direct messages, 07 Stripe subscriptions + creator support, 08 invite-only onboarding) plus image-first (Instagram) and video-first (TikTok) variants. Each prompt carries the faithful source prompt in a fenced block plus skill-integration notes (what to verify, which lane/artifact it feeds, web-vs-native and Stripe-vs-IAP reconciliation, moderation-as-launch-gate).

Wires the layer into SKILL.md (new Start Here step 1a archetype detection + a When-To-Load rule), adds an extensibility contract for future archetype packs under templates/app-archetypes/, and locks the behavior with a social-network-archetype-prompt-pack agent-behavior eval so detect-archetype -> AskUserQuestion -> load-pack -> sequence-prompts is enforced rather than prose-only.

Adds the Premium Mobile Craft lane: the invisible details that make a launched app feel premium rather than flashy, baked into the boilerplate. New references/premium-mobile-craft.md encodes the doctrine (premium = the compounding effect of fifty invisible decisions, not wild animation or heavy blur) across the five details — press states & spring physics, subtle/purposeful motion in the 150-300ms band, haptics discipline (confirm decisions/state changes only, never navigation/scroll/idle taps), keyboard behavior (input/submit never covered, intentional dismissal), and loading/empty states (size-preserving skeleton/shimmer over bare spinners; empty states that explain what/why + one action). Platform routing makes SwiftUI/latest Swift the primary target (with iOS 26 Liquid Glass affordances for chrome) and gives React Native (pressto/react-native-ease/react-native-pulsar/react-native-keyboard-controller) and Flutter parity, all driven from the shared DesignTokens.Motion scale and honoring Reduce Motion.

Ships templates/design-system/PremiumCraft.swift as drop-in SwiftUI boilerplate: a PremiumPressStyle button style (spring press feedback + optional confirming haptic), PremiumMotion springs derived from the tokenized motion scale, a .premiumAnimation(_:value:) modifier that collapses under Reduce Motion, a semantic Haptics layer (declarative .sensoryFeedback(for:trigger:) plus imperative one-shots), keyboard helpers (interactive drag-to-dismiss, tap/swipe focus-blur, a Done key-row), and a ShimmerModifier/.skeleton(isLoading)/EmptyStateView for loading and empty states. The file imports no framer-motion (check:template-safety stays green) and consumes the generated DesignTokens.Motion contract rather than hardcoding durations.

Wires the lane into SKILL.md routing (new When-To-Load rule) and design-visual-system.md (routing step 6a), extends templates/ux-patterns/UX_PATTERNS.md with a Premium Craft Details table, bug traps, and an acceptance-checklist item (kept the check:ux-patterns required phrases intact), and registers the authoritative Apple HIG/SwiftUI source URLs (Playing haptics, Motion, Loading, Applying Liquid Glass to custom views) in source-registry.yaml.

Adds native iOS proof awareness for Codex Desktop and CLI users. references/xcodebuildmcp-testing.md is now the native iOS proof hub: Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP tools are first-class when exposed (session_show_defaults before build/run/test, build_run_sim/test/screenshot/log proof recorded), SnapshotPreviews is supported for preview-only XCTest PNG/JSON exports through TEST_RUNNER_SNAPSHOTS_EXPORT_DIR, and serve-sim is supported for browser-visible booted-simulator streaming/control at routes such as http://localhost:3200. The guidance explicitly separates simulator/preview proof from backend/provider proof and Apple signing/distribution readiness.

Audit hardening: check-native-ios-proof no longer accepts SnapshotPreviews as the raw real-app screenshot capture route by itself. Ready iOS screenshot packets must name a real app, simulator, or device capture route such as MobAI, Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP, serve-sim, on-device capture, or real app capture, and any SnapshotPreviews support evidence must state its preview-only limitation. Validator fixtures cover both SnapshotPreviews-only screenshots and missing SnapshotPreviews limitations.

Ships check-native-ios-proof plus a native-ios-proof-route-skipped LaunchBench scenario and failure card. The validator checks iOS readiness claims for a named native proof route, Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP tool evidence, SnapshotPreviews export paths and preview-only limits, serve-sim URL/booted-simulator evidence and limits, provider-proof pairing, and the App Store signing/distribution limitation.

Threads the new native iOS proof standard through generated business templates and maintainer docs: PRODUCTION_READINESS.md gains a Native iOS Proof matrix, SCREENSHOTS.md and CONTENT_ASSETS.md recognize Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP/SnapshotPreviews/serve-sim inputs, PROJECT_STATE.yaml tracks the three tool routes, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md role templates tell future agents how to use them, and source-registry.yaml tracks getsentry/SnapshotPreviews, EmergeTools/SnapshotPreviews, and EvanBacon/serve-sim for weekly freshness.

Adds the Claude-vs-Codex runtime split and Claude Dynamic Workflows lane for the pre-build stages. New references/dynamic-workflows.md documents, for both agents and humans, when to use a Dynamic Workflow over inline/subagents/Compound Engineering, the ultracode and /effort ultracode and /deep-research triggers (Claude Code v2.1.154+ research preview), the agent()/parallel()/pipeline() API with per-agent model and worktree/remote isolation, the runtime limits (16 concurrent / 1000 total agents, no mid-run input, in-session resume), the six patterns mapped to each pre-build stage, token discipline (/goal, /loop, budgets), the quarantine pattern for untrusted reviews/social/scraped input, and save/ship-as-skill — with Claude owning research-through-spec and Codex owning the core app build.

Wires the lane into SKILL.md (new Claude vs Codex Routing And Dynamic Workflows contract section, a When-To-Load rule, and an Operating Posture bullet), cross-links parallel-agent-orchestration.md (Subagents Vs Dynamic Workflows note plus the workflows doc in its source basis), and threads the runtime split into the generated business-repo templates/repo-agent-entrypoints/AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md so launched repos keep the pre-build-on-Claude / build-on-Codex discipline. Registers the Claude Code workflows docs and the Anthropic launch writeup in source-registry.yaml.

Frames the Claude-vs-Codex split as a recommendation and default bias, not a hard requirement: the skill never blocks, gates, or refuses work in whichever runtime is in front of it. Adds a Detect Codex And Recommend Claude For Pre-Build behavior in dynamic-workflows.md (runtime-detection signals plus a recommend-once-never-block protocol) so Codex users on pre-build stages get the Claude+Dynamic-Workflows tip surfaced once, recorded in PROJECT_STATE.yaml, then the work continues in the current runtime with the same fan-out/adversarial-verification/quarantine shapes. Softens the wording across SKILL.md, README.md, and the business-repo AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md templates accordingly, and extends the runtime_split state contract with current_runtime/recommended_*/handled_by/recommendation_made fields.

Verifies the existing RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2026 figures in the monetization/onboarding anti-pattern digest against the source SOSA 2026 PDF (all cited numbers — 10.7% vs 2.1% hard-vs-freemium D35, $2.32 vs $0.27 D14 RPI, $35.89 vs $6.67 / $62.19 vs $10.69 price-tier LTV, 4.6% reach $10K with ~75% drop-off from $1K, the 25.5/37.4/42.5% trial-length curve, and the Day-0 cancellation figures — confirmed accurate) and extends the digest with three findings the report carried but the skill had not captured.

revenue-monetization.md gains §8a Billing Health And Involuntary Churn (Google Play ~31% of cancellations are involuntary billing failures vs ~14% on the App Store; recovery system = grace period/account hold, RevenueCat billing-issue webhook -> dunning push/in-app banner/payment.failed email, one-tap update-payment, and split voluntary-vs-involuntary churn analytics), an AI-retention evidence bullet in §4c (~41% higher revenue per payer but ~30% faster churn), and two new §10 anti-patterns (#13 treat involuntary churn as inevitable; #14 bank on AI novelty for retention). onboarding-conversion.md Plan And Trial Mix gains the paid-intro-offer-as-free-trial-alternative finding (only ~9.3% of apps use promo offers; ~30% of new subs enter via intro discount; hobby apps over-rely at 65-99% vs top performers 0-10%). source-registry.yaml records the 2026-06-05 verification. No new external source URLs introduced; figures framed as defaults to TEST, with pricing/plan/trial/paywall-model changes still founder-gated.

Adds three further SOSA 2026 findings verified against the source PDF: (1) revenue-monetization.md §4c gains the first-renewal-inflection evidence bullet (~half of monthly and ~3-in-4 yearly subs don't pass renewal #1; +18-30pp jump to renewal #2; yearly retains ~3-10x weekly; Y1 retention softening annual ~31%->28% / monthly ~10%->8%; win by accelerating time-to-value before renewal #1, not by gating harder); (2) new §8b Reactivation And Win-Back (reactivation is plan-dependent: monthly ~18-24% / weekly ~7-10% / annual ~4-6% with annual churn largely permanent; reactivation fires when the problem recurs not when the email lands; offer pause-instead-of-cancel and one-tap return; plan-duration-segmented reactivation analytics); (3) §6 gains the web-revenue-adoption signal (~41% of top-tier apps have web revenue vs ~1.3% hobby, ~31x gap; web ~3.2% of revenue globally) plus web-funnel design notes (sell the problem before the solution; discounted paid trial outperforms free trial on web; run web vs app-install creative separately). No new external source URLs.

Expands the Launch Narrative & Cadence lane with the agency's full operational 'X Launch Playbook & Checklist' (sourced from the shared doc): references/launch-narrative-cadence.md gains the six-phase checklist (Foundation bold claim/outcome-over-feature/network/activatable communities/roadmap-as-content; Outlier Research with the 500+/50+/50+ ~10x heuristic; Content Creation write-every-post-yourself, format-matching, launch-video rules, and a defensible rage-bait line gated by ethics-guardrail.md; Amplification Roster budget/vetting bands; the Launch-Day Timing Engine three gates; and the Follow-Up Window), the Sourcing-vs-Ranking algorithm model, a Launch-Killing Mistakes list, Final Principles, and the agency's stated success bands (~2-10M views / 500-2,000+ comments / 1,000-5,000+ retweets) framed as aim-at targets, not promises.

Turns templates/growth/LAUNCH_NARRATIVE.md into a real six-phase checklist artifact and extends check:launch-narrative to require the new spine (Bold Claim, Outcome Over Feature, Marketing At The Table, Outlier Research, Amplification Roster, Launch-Day Timing Engine, Follow-Up Window, Sourcing And Ranking) on top of the existing copy guardrails. Keeps claims hygiene: the agency's headline aggregates (100M+ views, $1M+ execution, 2-3x reply uplift) are marked agency-stated and must not be repeated as the launching company's own results; no new external source URLs are introduced.

Adds a research-first localization lane: localization is now a market-selection decision made from search-demand evidence, not a translate-everything task. New references/localization-market-research.md defines the Demand -> Prioritize -> Localize -> Measure method, maps the 'keyword popularity > X / low difficulty / localization priority per country' research onto the skill's existing stack (AppKittie keyword popularity/difficulty, Apple Search Ads, App Store Connect App Analytics, XPOZ) with no TryAstro/MCP dependency, and ranks markets into Tier 1 full-localize / Tier 2 metadata-only / Tier 3 defer that drive effort across every surface (store metadata/keywords/screenshots, paywall/offers, landing+hreflang/llms.txt, lifecycle email, and paid storefronts).

Ships templates/localization-market-research/ (LOCALIZATION_MARKET_RESEARCH.md market opportunity matrix + localization-market-research.html board), wires the lane into SKILL.md Phase 1 research/ASO routing/load-rules/Deliverable Standard, tracks it as research-lane evidence in PROJECT_STATE.yaml, and cross-links aso-store-ops.md, app-store-listing-prep.md, paid-user-acquisition.md, surfaces-b2c.md, change-cascade.md, geo-seo.md, and launch-coverage.md.

Adds the check-localization-research validator (translate-first guard plus required priority tiers, per-storefront popularity/difficulty signals, and demand-research tool sourcing), wires it into run-validator-fixtures (pass + translate-first fail cases), and adds the localization-translate-first-without-demand-research LaunchBench scenario so the translate-first miss is regression-covered, not prose-only.

Adds the Launch Narrative & Cadence lane: a public-launch playbook that treats every release as an event. references/launch-narrative-cadence.md encodes the tentpole-vs-weekly-heartbeat model, algorithm compounding / standing audience, marketing-at-the-table roadmap influence, the launch-day run-of-show (post order and who replies to what), and feeling-first copy craft. It is grounded in verified launch artifacts (Koji by Brilliant ~4.71M impressions, Moda ~4.50M, ChatGPT 'Paperwork' ~2.66M) and the Shown Media agency's stated method, with explicit caveats marking the MrBeast-alumni, 10B-views, CEO-Emmy, and 'many startups died' claims as unverified and not to be repeated.

Ships growth/LAUNCH_NARRATIVE.md as the per-business artifact and a deterministic check:launch-narrative validator enforcing the 2026 copy guardrails (no hashtags, no emojis, no link in the main announcement post) against fenced post copy, plus required sections, a named emotional angle, and traceability refs. Wires the lane into SKILL.md routing, the growth lane evidence in templates/PROJECT_STATE.yaml, the root/runtime audit chains, README validator list, source-registry.yaml (Shown Media, the verified launch posts, Brilliant Koji), and a launch-narrative-feeling-first-missing LaunchBench scenario.

Adds a monetization-and-growth anti-pattern digest so agents know the cozy defaults NOT to reach for, grounded in the RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2026 report (no new source URLs; reuses the registered state-of-subscription-apps entry). revenue-monetization.md gains a new §10 digest index plus detailed traps (assume you are the exception; soft paywall by reflex; push monthly/skip annual; price low to be 'humble'); onboarding-conversion.md gains Conversion Anti-Patterns (short-trial reflex; ignore Day 0 and bank on a day-30 win-back; one experiment a year); paid-user-acquisition.md gains Anti-Patterns (run ads before you can read the result; optimize creative before the paywall; chase vanity views); app-store-listing-prep.md frames localization as a high-leverage conversion lever (not optional translation) and cross-links the existing App Preview lever; viral-growth-loops.md cross-links the vanity-views trap. Each benchmark is framed as a default to TEST, not dogma, and pricing/plan/trial/paywall-model changes stay founder-gated.

Adds the monetization-cozy-default-stack-unexamined LaunchBench scenario (validated by check-onboarding-conversion) and a launchbench-evals.md catalog entry so the default-stack miss is regression-covered, not prose-only.

Tier 1/2/3 proof hardening across three lanes — revenue, attribution, store-screenshots — eliminating the self-attestable 1-byte bypass. Revenue: probe-revenuecat.ts writes a non-secret revenue/revenuecat-proof.json via RevenueCat REST API v2 (offerings + entitlements); check-revenue.ts validates fingerprint (revenuecat@1), freshness (30-day warn), non-empty offering_id and entitlement_ids, byte-identity and sub-300-byte stub rejection, and bare-word evidence path warnings; templates/revenue/ ships the example artifact and prose companion. Attribution: probe-posthog.ts writes analytics/posthog-proof.json via PostHog HogQL Query API (with events-list fallback); check-attribution-contract.ts validates fingerprint (posthog@1), event_count > 0, 72-hour freshness, example-identity and sub-80-byte stub rejection, and bare-word/https-URL evidence path warnings. Screenshots: check-store-screenshots.ts adds PNG IHDR binary parser (reads 29 bytes: verifies signature, color_type for alpha, dimensions against ASC_WELL_DIMENSIONS lookup table), producer-not-verifier enforcement (builder/grader root fields, grader must differ from builder, per-slot grader_notes minimum, suspicious_perfect warning), and Tier-1 example-copy/sub-200-byte stub rejection gated behind laneIsActive. Shared: launch-state.ts exports validateReason, REASON_MIN_LENGTH, STALL_STALE_DAYS, and helpers for dated non-trivial stall reason enforcement in validate-project-state.ts and check-lane-coverage.ts. Settings.json hooks in templates/repo-agent-entrypoints: SCREENSHOTS.md and PROJECT_STATE.yaml hooks are blocking; proof-artifact Write/Edit and Bash hooks advisory; final-PNG hook mandates a separate grader pass. Both package.json files register probe:revenuecat and probe:posthog outside the audit pipeline. Source-registry.yaml gains 6 missing entries for the new probe file URLs.

Public-readiness pass: adds an MIT LICENSE and license metadata, removes hardcoded author machine paths/username from AGENTS.md, references/skill-versioning.md, and check-skill-version.ts (now uses the B2C_SKILL_SOURCE env override with a repo-relative fallback), and makes validate:skill skip gracefully when the maintainer-only skill-creator linter is not installed so a fresh clone can run npm run audit end to end.

Adds a README "Who this is for & what you need" section that states the subscription-app scope, the Claude Code/Codex runtime requirement, and discloses that third-party provider accounts are optional and swappable; registers the Claude Code product URL in source-registry.yaml.

Adds the Business Control workspace read-model schema plus a deterministic renderer/checker that adapts state/business.json and PROJECT_STATE.yaml into state/workspace.generated.json, validated by check:business-control-plane-workspace.

Wires the Business Control workspace adapter into root/runtime package scripts, audit gates, README/AGENTS/control-plane docs, and prototype query-param loading so JSON-backed local experiments stay reproducible.

Adds validator-backed Session Continuity: generated business AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/APP_AGENTS.md/orchestrator prompts now force resume/status/handoff work to reconstruct state from PROJECT_STATE.yaml, launch-cockpit.html, ORCHESTRATION.md, PRODUCTION_READINESS.md, FAILURE_CARDS.md, and git status before choosing work. Adds continuity state fields, ORCHESTRATION.md continuity handoff slots, check-continuity-contract, validator fixtures, LaunchBench coverage, and an agent-behavior eval for continuity drift.

Post-commit audit hardening: restores source-root maintainer parity for 11-star/emotional-design checks, adds package/version parity enforcement, makes emotional_design a required state lane, tightens emotional-design board and live-claim proof validation, makes Design Room versioning render/check before commit, requires exact template evidence paths, makes iOS App Preview 1 founder-deferral explicit, wires BRAND.md and DEMO_VIDEO.md into starter artifacts and agent entrypoints, refreshes runtime-install documentation, and removes stale command/reference drift.

In-depth audit pass (5-dimension review): hardens check-emotional-design.ts (derives scarcity/social-proof attestation from parsed card blocks instead of stripped text, fixing a false positive; implements the previously-undocumented spend-prompt-near-reward scan; extends dark-pattern phrase scans to ONBOARDING/SPEC/APP_STORE_LISTING; expands confirmshaming/commitment-guilt patterns; reads EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md in the children scan and makes under-13 an error). Fixes check-store-screenshots.ts (unreachable board-missing check now gated to done; count regex matches 'N screenshots').

Reconciles documentation to the real validator: ethics-guardrail.md §3/§5 now list the actually-enforced attestation fields and the emotional_design.* rule codes (the old check-ethics-guardrail.ts codes were stale); adds EU AI Act Art. 5 and California AADC to the regulatory landscape; canonicalizes all PostHog event names (perceived_effort_*, peak_moment_reached, intent_mirror_continued, commitment_made); repoints the 12 card Spec Pointers to experience-cards.md; corrects Schultz (Science 1997) and IKEA-effect (Journal of Consumer Psychology) citations; fixes the emotional-design.html curve so it renders (concrete stub coordinates), axis label, and headings.

Elevates the App Store app preview video to a first-class growth lever (verified against Apple docs: up to 3 previews precede screenshots, the first autoplays muted as a ~5-second silent hook with a poster frame that must sell on its own). SCREENSHOTS.md now requires an App Preview Video (Autoplay Hook) section and an Asset Knowledge Brief so both screenshots and the preview are engineered from RESEARCH.md (user/problem), 11_STAR_EXPERIENCE.md (magical moment), and EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md (Emotional North Star + Experience Card), and are always produced through the screenshot ASO skill (ParthJadhav/app-store-screenshots / ios-screenshots / app-preview-video). check-store-screenshots enforces this and it is auditable for existing apps; adds the app-preview-hook-missing-or-generic failure card and the app-preview-video-hook-missing LaunchBench eval.

Wires the Emotional Experience System into the downstream artifact templates: ANALYTICS.md (emotion-card event catalog + Dark-Pattern Watch), DESIGN.md (three-level tone + per-card motion/reduced-motion spec), TECH_SPEC.md (Experience Card frontstage/backstage + perceived-effort step-to-operation map), PRODUCTION_READINESS.md (per-card bright-line evidence rows), and ONBOARDING.md (card-timing contract).

Restructures EMOTIONAL_AUDIT.md per-journey tables from a 12-column block into a readable 8-column table plus a Findings & Pathway sub-block per journey, adds per-journey Emotional Curve markers for all 10 journeys, caps step scores at 7-star, corrects the lane key to lanes.emotional_design, and strengthens the acceptance gate to cover Critical and High journeys.

Adds the Emotional Experience System lane: an emotionally-intelligent consumer-product design layer grounded in behavioral science (Skinner, Schultz, Eyal, Fogg, Cialdini, Buell-Norton, Kahneman) with a 12-card Experience Card deck led by the four named cards — Commitment, Variable Reward, Perceived Effort Delay, and Intent Mirroring.

Ships a Producer path (charge a feature with engineered emotion → EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md + emotional-design.html) and an Auditor path (walk every user journey on-device, score it through a six-lens review, and return a pathway to a better state → EMOTIONAL_AUDIT.md).

Adds the check:emotional-design validator enforcing the artifact contract plus a deterministic dark-pattern guardrail: high-risk cards (variable reward, streak, scarcity, urgency, social proof) require an ethics attestation, a user-control escape hatch, a counter-metric, and a mechanism-specific truthfulness proof; live copy is scanned for fake scarcity, fabricated social proof, confirmshaming, and commitment guilt.

Wires the lane into SKILL.md routing, launch-coverage, PROJECT_STATE.yaml (lanes.emotional_design), failure cards, and LaunchBench, and threads the cards through the 11-star 6/7-star levels, onboarding-conversion timing, and analytics-attribution events.

Producer/verifier follow-up + business-repo plumbing. Screenshot rubric ledger v1.2: builder/grader are now structured {agent,session_id} objects, a grading_pass {separate_pass,started_at,method} attestation and per-slot observed_evidence are required, and check-store-screenshots.ts ERRORs (done)/WARNs (partial) when grading is not a separate pass, when builder.session_id===grader.session_id, or when observed_evidence/grader_notes are missing. New scripts/grade-screenshots.ts is the only supported write path for the separate vision grading pass (emits a per-slot grading task with real PNG IHDR dimensions; validates provenance before writing the ledger). settings.json final-PNG hook is now BLOCKING (exits 1) and routes to grade-screenshots.ts; its jq/SKILL_ROOT prerequisites are documented. Honest limit recorded throughout: a single deliberate agent can still fabricate two session ids — founder review of the PNGs alongside the ledger remains the ultimate backstop. Plumbing: PROVIDER_PROOF.md PostHog/RevenueCat evidence rows now point at the .json probe artifacts; repo-agent-entrypoints/AGENTS.md documents the hook prerequisites, the Doppler-run probes (POSTHOG_PERSONAL_API_KEY/REVENUECAT_SECRET_API_KEY), and the separate grading pass.

Grading residual hardening (rubric v1.2.1): the final-PNG grading hook now also intercepts Bash writes (cp/mv/tee/install/ditto/sips/magick/convert and >/>> redirection) targeting screenshots/final/*.png, not just Write/Edit. check-store-screenshots tightens the OPTIMIZED provenance floor — grader_notes >=30 chars, observed_evidence >=15 chars, a new distinctness check (identical normalized observed_evidence across slots ERRORs at done / WARNs at partial, with a leading slot-N: label stripped so the prefix trick cannot defeat it), and an exact-match generic-phrase guard on multi-word clichés (no false positives on concrete observations that merely begin with a common adjective). Honest limit documented: the Bash regex misses piped-tee/variable-path/rsync/curl, and a creative grader can still write distinct slot-specific fiction — founder review of the PNGs alongside the ledger remains the backstop.
